title: Wire canonicalize_display_name into SET_NICKNAME and GROUP_CREATE handlers
description: Why: enforcement must happen at write time before storage and broadcast. What: in relay/src/ws.rs: (1) SET_NICKNAME handler (~line 483): replace current 'let nickname = params.nickname.trim().to_string()' + separate length check with 'let nickname = match canonicalize_display_name(&params.nickname) { Ok(n) => n, Err(e) => { send_client_error(..., rpc_errors::INVALID_REQUEST, e).await; continue; } }' — remove the old length check (it is now inside canonicalize_display_name); (2) GROUP_CREATE handler (~line 866): before create_group call, add 'let name = match canonicalize_display_name(&params.name) { Ok(n) => n, Err(e) => { send_client_error(..., rpc_errors::INVALID_REQUEST, e).await; continue; } }' then use 'name' instead of 'params.name' in create_group and GroupCreateResult. Defensive rules: both handlers must return error to client on Err, never store invalid names, no unwrap().
acceptance_criteria: cargo test --workspace passes; SET_NICKNAME with RTL char returns error; GROUP_CREATE with ZWS strips it before storage
close_reason: canonicalize_display_name wired into SET_NICKNAME and GROUP_CREATE; all tests pass
